President Howard Elliott Of the
Northern Pacific says that the in
spection trip Of James J. Hill has
resulted in opening the eyes of the
eastern men in the party to thN
growth and importance of WasS
ington and that this will mean the
opening of the purse strings of
Wall street and the investment Of
additional money in the northwest.
The representatives of the eastern
financial interests were brought
here, he said to show them that the
confidence of Hill in the future OF
the northwest was not misplaced.
and Elliott ventured the opinion
that most of the fasteners are re-
turning much impressed.


Mr.. Elliott said that nothing is
under contemplation at this time
in the way of grade separation -
the N. p. and that the report th1>
N. P. trains would be sent over the
G. N. tracks, abandoning the line
through the heart Of the city, is a
mistake.
